title: Echitella perrieri
taxonomy: Echitella perrieri is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Echitella, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to Madagascar. The family Apocynaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes over 4,000 species of vines, shrubs, and trees. Echitella perrieri is a member of the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants that are characterized by their five-merous flowers. The classification of Echitella perrieri is further supported by its unique floral and morphological characteristics.
uses: Echitella perrieri is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Madagascar. Its roots and leaves are used in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin ailments.
